entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., the crop studied for root length density and seed yield under drought stress

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH DENSITY
entities.description: A measure of root system variability used to acquire water, specifically evaluated in the vegetative growth stage

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The amount of seed produced by the chickpea plant

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT
entities.description: Water stress occurring during the terminal stage of crop growth

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: Conditions characterized by a lack of available soil water

entities.name: PVC CYLINDER SCREENING
entities.description: A procedure using tall PVC cylinders to facilitate the screening of genotypes with large root length density

entities.name: FIELD TRIALS
entities.description: Experiments conducted in the field to evaluate genotypes under water deficit conditions

entities.name: VEGETATIVE GROWTH STAGE
entities.description: The period of plant growth before flowering

entities.name: FIELD
entities.description: The outdoor environment where the chickpea genotypes were grown

relationships.description: Significant positive correlation and contribution to yield under drought
Helps the plant cope with water stress
The crop for which root length density is measured
The crop for which seed yield is measured
Used as a method to screen for genotypes with large RLD
Results from cylinder trials correlate with field trial RLD
The crop subjected to terminal drought stress
Affects the seed yield of the crop
